pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly creates this file with the mission requirements.
task.json instructions and export_result.sh path variables.
task.json instructions and export_result.sh path variables.
Mathematical calculation: Mars radius (3396.19 km) + 400 km target altitude.
Mathematical derivation of SSO inclination using Mars J2 (0.00196) and Year length (686.9 days) yields ~92.9 deg.
Mathematical derivation of SSO inclination using Mars J2 yields ~92.9 deg.
Mathematical calculation for RAAN drift over 60 Earth days (360 degrees / 686.973 days * 60 days) yields ~31.44 deg.
Mathematical calculation for RAAN drift over 60 Earth days (360 degrees / 686.973 days * 60 days) yields ~31.44 deg.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates entirely on synthetic data dynamically generated via setup scripts. Mathematical analysis confirms the required Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO) inclination for a 400 km Mars orbit is approximately 92.9 degrees (expected range 92.8 - 93.0) and the Right Ascension of the Ascending Node (RAAN) drift over 60 Earth days is approximately 31.4 degrees (expected range 30.5 - 32.5), using the provided Mars J2 perturbation value of 0.00196045.
